estimated.note: Higher estimate includes unreported deaths in restricted areas
Slight variance due to delayed military casualty reports

parties.name: Iranian Civilians

estimated.note: Wide range: HRANA says 7,015; Iranian doctors network says 16,500-18,000; Iran International estimates up to 36,500. Trump cited 32,000 in State of the Union.

parties.arrestedNote: Mass arrests during Dec 2025–Jan 2026 protests. HRANA documented 41,800+ detained across 31 provinces.

parties.name: Iran

confirmed.source: Hengaw 6th report (March 20, Day 21): 5,900 killed — 5,305 military, 595 civilians. Includes 127 minors, 168 women. 204 children killed per Al Jazeera tracker. Iran Red Crescent: 1,400+ total killed, 18,000+ injured.

estimated.source: HRANA (Human Rights Activists in Iran) — March 17 report. 1,354 civilians, 1,138 military, 622 unclassified = 3,114 total. Near-6,000 attacks recorded nationwide.
estimated.note: HRANA (Human Rights Activists News Agency) — March 16 report. 1,351 civilians, 1,126 military, 622 unclassified. Total 3,099. Nearly 6,000 attacks recorded nationwide. By Day 4 alone, HRANA had counted 1,097 civilians killed (including 181 children). 65 schools and 77 healthcare facilities damaged. 42,000+ civilian sites damaged per Iranian government spokesperson. Internet at 4% — independent verification near-impossible. ~40 officials killed including Armed Forces CoS Bagheri, IRGC commanders Salami, Rashid, Hajizadeh.

rumored.note: Hengaw (March 20): 5,900 killed — 5,305 military, 595 civilians (127 minors, 168 women) across 25 provinces. IDF Brig Gen Defrin claims 6,000+ IRGC killed and 15,000+ wounded. With ongoing Nowruz strikes (Day 21) adding more casualties, upper range approaching 9,000. Internet blackout prevents independent verification.

socialSources.claim: 5th report (March 17): 5,300 killed — 4,789 military, 511 civilians. 120 minors, 160 women among dead. 178 cities across 25 provinces affected.
By Day 4 alone: 1,097 civilians killed (including 181 children), 5,402 injured. This was already 3x the government's claimed toll at that point.
IDF claims 6,000+ IRGC killed, 15,000 wounded, 70% of Iran's missile launchers destroyed. Plans through Passover (3+ more weeks).
IDF spokesperson claims approximately 1,900 Iranian regime members 'eliminated' as of Day 18.
Experts say actual death toll 'could be far greater as more details are released' — Iran's internet blackout prevents verification.

parties.injuredNote: 18,000+ injured per Health Ministry / Al Jazeera tracker. 77 healthcare facilities damaged. HRANA counted 5,402 injured by Day 4 alone.
parties.missingNote: 101 sailors from IRINS Dena — only 79 of 180 crew recovered after ship sank in Gulf of Oman.
parties.displacedNote: 3.2M displaced across Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz and border regions per UNHCR. Shelter capacity exceeded.
parties.arrestedNote: 20 arrested in NW Iran for alleged cooperation with Israel. 18 arrested for ties to Iran International (March 16-17).

parties.name: Israel

confirmed.source: IDF, Israeli government, and Magen David Adom. 13 soldiers KIA (includes 7 killed Beit Shemesh synagogue missile, 2 West Bank soldiers, others). 7+ civilians killed: Yehud cluster bomb (2), Ramat Gan (2), Thai worker Moshav Adanim (1), others. 2 lightly wounded in Rehovot (Day 21 Nowruz barrage). Fragment from intercepted missile struck near Dung Gate, Jerusalem Old City (400m from Western Wall).

estimated.source: Israeli media compilation including Alma Center daily reports
estimated.note: 23 confirmed killed (Alma Center March 19 update + Israeli emergency services). Key incidents: 9 killed in Beit Shemesh synagogue (Day 2), 2 killed in Yehud cluster bomb (Day 10), 2 killed in Ramat Gan IRGC retaliation (Day 18), 1 Thai worker Moshav Adanim (Day 20), 1 in Tel Aviv (Day 1). 4 wounded in Kiryat Shmona (Day 20). 5 additional wounded from cluster barrages. Estimated 25 total including 2 additional unreported deaths in restricted military zones.

rumored.note: Iranian state media claims significantly higher Israeli casualties — IRGC claims 40+ killed across 9 missile salvos. OSINT analysts report direct hits on residential areas in southern Tel Aviv not covered by Israeli media. Israel's military censor restricts casualty reporting under wartime regulations.

socialSources.claim: Multiple videos show direct hits on residential areas in southern Tel Aviv not covered by Israeli media — possible casualties suppressed by military censorship.
IRGC claims 40+ Israelis killed across 9 missile salvos — IDF only confirming 17.
2 killed in Ramat Gan as Iranian cluster missile hits multiple impact sites. Shrapnel rained on several neighborhoods.

parties.injuredNote: 3,802+ injured per Alma Center Day 22 update. 2 lightly wounded in Rishon Lezion from Day 22 cluster bomb. 2 lightly wounded in Rehovot from Nowruz barrage cluster bomb (Day 21). 4 wounded in Kiryat Shmona March 19. Additional wounds from Jerusalem Old City fragment.

parties.name: United States

confirmed.source: Pentagon / NPR / Time Magazine. 7 killed by enemy fire + 6 KC-135 crew killed in crash. ~200 wounded per CENTCOM (March 17).

estimated.note: 13 confirmed KIA: 6 in Kuwait drone strike (Port Shuaiba, March 1) — Capt. Khork, SFC Tietjens, SFC Amor, Sgt. Coady identified. 1 first-day combat death (March 1). 6 KC-135 crew in Iraq crash (March 12) — Maj. Klinner identified. ~200 wounded per CENTCOM (March 17). 4th manned US aircraft lost.

rumored.note: IRGC claims 650+ US killed/wounded in first 2 days (largely debunked by Jerusalem Post). Ali Larijani claimed US soldiers were captured — Pentagon explicitly denied. Iran state TV broadcast alleged captured US troops. Multiple IRGC claims later deleted after being disproven.

socialSources.claim: IRGC spokesperson claimed 650 US troops killed or wounded in first 2 days, including 160 at US military HQ in Bahrain. Claims largely disproven.
Claims Iran captured US soldiers. Pentagon explicitly denied any captures.
Sources in Bahrain report 12+ additional US wounded at 5th Fleet HQ not in official Pentagon count.

parties.injuredNote: ~200 wounded per CENTCOM (March 17). 180+ returned to duty, 8 severely injured. Includes casualties from drone attacks, IED strikes, and KC-135 crash.

parties.name: Lebanon

confirmed.source: Lebanese Health Ministry (March 20, Day 21): 1,001 killed, 2,584 wounded. Over 1 million displaced (1 in 5 Lebanese). UN warned nearly a fifth of Lebanon's population uprooted.

estimated.source: Lebanese media + IDF Hezbollah fighter estimates
estimated.note: 968 confirmed by Health Ministry (March 18). 35+ killed on Day 19 across Baalbek, Sahmar, Beirut. Al Manar TV director killed. IDF destroyed 2 Litani River bridges, trapping ~150,000 civilians. Ground invasion ongoing south of Litani.

rumored.note: Hezbollah fighter casualties not separately broken out in Health Ministry figures — actual Hezbollah dead could be 300-400+. IDF ground invasion (launched March 16) expected to significantly increase casualties. Rescue teams unable to access multiple strike sites in southern Lebanon. Some villages entirely cut off.

socialSources.claim: 26 killed in last 24 hours across Beirut, Nabatieh, Baalbek, Bint Jbeil. 1 million displaced — 20% of Lebanon's population.
One million displaced nationwide. 130,000+ in 600+ collective shelters. Humanitarian access increasingly restricted.
IDF claims approximately 200 Hezbollah fighters killed since March 2. Hezbollah deliberately does not announce its dead.

parties.injuredNote: 2,584+ wounded per Lebanese Health Ministry (March 19). 79 women, 118 children, 40 healthcare workers among dead.
parties.displacedNote: 1,049,328 displaced per Lebanese authorities as of March 20 — approximately 1 in 5 Lebanese. UN warned nearly a fifth of the population is uprooted.

parties.name: Gulf States (UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain, etc.)

confirmed.source: Al Jazeera tracker (March 18). 21 killed across Gulf states: UAE 8 (2 military + 6 foreign nationals), Saudi Arabia 4, Kuwait 5, Bahrain 2, other 2.

estimated.source: Compiled from regional government reports and HRW (March 17)
estimated.note: Kuwait: 11 killed (6 US soldiers + 5 Kuwaiti), 99 injured. UAE: 8 killed (2 military, 6 foreign nationals), 157 injured, 262 missiles detected (241 destroyed), ~1,400 drones intercepted. Saudi Arabia: 2 killed (al-Kharj), 12 injured. Bahrain: 2 killed (1 Asian worker, 1 Bahraini woman), 8+ injured. Oman: 3 killed. Iraq (non-US/French): ~27 killed in US strikes on militia targets + crossfire.

rumored.note: HRW reports majority of Gulf civilian victims are migrant workers — deaths may be underreported due to foreign national status. Iraq casualties from US strikes on militia targets (27+) not fully tracked. Some Gulf nations restrict casualty reporting. Air defense interception rates (92-95%) prevented much worse toll.

socialSources.claim: At least 11 civilian deaths and 268 injuries across GCC states. 10 of those killed are foreign nationals. Migrant worker deaths likely underreported.
Fujairah oil hub struck 2 consecutive days. Saudi al-Kharj compound hit with casualties. Full picture unclear as some nations restrict reporting.

parties.injuredNote: UAE: 158 injured (updated March 19). Kuwait: 99 (Day 21 Mina Al-Ahmadi strike reported no new casualties). Saudi Arabia: 12. Bahrain: 8+. HRW documented 268+ civilian injuries across GCC states.

confirmed.source: French Ministry of Armed Forces (March 12). WO Arnaud Frion, 7th Alpine Chasseurs Battalion, killed in drone strike near Makhmour, Iraq. First non-US NATO fatality.

estimated.note: WO Arnaud Frion killed at French base near Makhmour, Iraq. 6 French soldiers total wounded. Macron condemned attack.
